note: Identical digit-emit/divide-by-10/reverse algorithm; the signed variant differs only by a negative-value branch dead for all v>=0, so it is the unsigned routine plus sign handling and the two coincide on the entire shared non-negative domain - a classic dedup target (real redis later made sdsll2str delegate to sdsull2str).
refuter: SUSTAINED: redis ll2string (src/util.c:391) already implements the merged form; the source comment itself says "Identical ... but for unsigned long long type".

note: Both are single-key-dict dispatchers that echo the input when the magic key is absent, but the transform branches are entirely different (self.tags[key].to_python(value[key]) vs X(obj["_foo"])), so agreement holds only on rows where both fall through to returning the input unchanged.

mapLegacyArgs inserts "content" into a CLI arg slice after "new" while skipCSSSpace strips one leading whitespace byte; any input triggering either transform diverges, so battery equality can only come from both returning the input unmodified.

On non-matching inputs both return 0, but the match branches return unrelated values (the equivalence-class char code vs fixed 5/2) and get_equi_class additionally advances *pp by side effect, so they are not the same computation.

diffgrep_consume returns 0/1 from a regex match on +/- diff lines while strbuf_expand_literal returns 0/1/3 and appends expanded escape chars to a strbuf; agreement is just the shared return-0 fallthrough on simple non-matching lines.

loadAsIndex searches a directory for index.<ext> files returning (PathPair,bool,*DifferentCase) while validateResolveExtensions validates or defaults an extension list returning []string; on the success/transform paths (found file, nil order default) they produce unrelated values, so only degenerate empty/echo rows can agree.

section_name_match parses a [section "sub"] header against a dotted name returning a consumed-length offset while is_conflict_marker checks for repeated =<>| markers returning a 0/1 flag; on any matching input the returned values differ (offset vs 1), so battery equality is just both returning 0 on non-matching simple lines.

checksumok validates a '$'-prefixed packet against a hex checksum returning bool while parseStopPacket parses T/W/X stop replies into a struct plus error; their domains and outputs are unrelated, so agreement can only be both rejecting/failing on the battery's simple inputs.

trimLeadingSlash strips a leading '/' (with path.Clean) and fixLongPath returns paths under 248 bytes unchanged, so both behave as identity on short slash-free strings; any "/x" input ('x' vs '/x') or long path diverges.

repairLastChunkFile does file I/O (open/read magic/remove corrupt file) on a map[int]string and determineColumnsToRender selects non-empty columns of a [][]string; only degenerate rows (empty map -> echo, empty data -> empty slice) can agree.

Both act as int identity except on disjoint special sets: may_get_selection maps '*'/'+' to 0 (or keeps them after a clip_get_selection side effect) while backslash_trans maps 'r','t','e','b' to control chars, so equality holds only for battery values outside both special sets.
